Name: Post 1 — The Hypothesis
Description:
**Article project: "Grammar as Protocol — How Formal Constraints Enable Agentic Security Research"**

A 2,000–2,500 word thought-leadership article composed as a Linear issue graph and exported via the Broadsheet publishing pipeline. Each issue body in this project is a section of prose; reading order is determined by within-milestone blocking relations.

**Thesis:** Formal grammars (specifically Pkl as constraint language) serve as universal protocol layers that enable reliable agent-to-agent coordination in high-stakes security research workflows, where traditional prompt engineering fails due to precision requirements.

**Audience:** Security researchers + agent-platform builders + soft contractor-pipeline signal.

**Substrate docs:** Agents on Rails (Pkl), Self-Reinforcing Cybernetic Loop, Operationalizing Autonomous Cyber-Physical Systems, Secure Agent Evaluation Platform Engineering.

**Parent scope:** Slice of the [Grammar as Protocol](<https://linear.app/tidelands-dev/project/grammar-as-protocol-9e59d728cfc6>) project. XState, Tree-sitter, Argo, and multi-vertical applicability live in the parent, not in this article.

**Publish gates:** [PLAN-213](https://linear.app/tidelands-dev/issue/PLAN-213/openai-codex-vs-code-extension-ctrlcmdclick-bypasses-link-confirmation) and [PLAN-78](https://linear.app/tidelands-dev/issue/PLAN-78/gemini-cli-predictable-temp-file-path-in-projectregistry-potential) disclosure or aging-out; pre-publish anonymization sufficiency check.
